content.text:
## Task: Fix [medium] Code Quality: mock-response.ts doc still claims coverage by a deleted characterization suite
## Step: review

### src/test-utils/mock-response.ts
```typescript
/**
 * Builds a minimal fetch `Response` stand-in for the mocked global `fetch`.
 *
 * `text()` returns the raw text (defaults to the JSON-stringified body).
 * `json()` returns the parsed body when one is provided; otherwise it falls
 * back to parsing `text` as JSON (when only `text` was given), and finally to
 * an empty object. `ok` is derived from `status` (true for any 2xx) unless an
 * explicit `ok` is supplied, in which case it wins.
 *
 * The `json()` method prefers an explicit `body` when supplied. When only
 * `text` was given, it parses the text as JSON; when neither is supplied it
 * falls back to an empty object `{}`. (No call site actually relies on this
 * branch: on the error path `ApiClient.request` only reads `.text()`, and on
 * the success path a `body` is always supplied.)
 *
 * Test-only utility: deliberately kept out of the production `tsc` build via
 * the `src/test-utils/**` glob in `tsconfig.json`'s `exclude` array, so it is
 * never shipped to `wwwroot/dist`. Only test files import it.
 */
export interface MockResponseOptions {
  ok?: boolean;
  status?: number;
  body?: unknown;
  text?: string;
}

export function mockResponse(opts: MockResponseOptions): Response {
  const status = opts.status ?? 200;
  const ok = opts.ok ?? (status >= 200 && status < 300);
  const text = opts.text ?? JSON.stringify(opts.body ?? {});
  return {
    ok,
    status,
    text: async () => text,
    json: async () => opts.body ?? (opts.text ? JSON.parse(opts.text) : {}),
  } as unknown as Response;
}

```
You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: Code Quality
Severity: medium
File: src/test-utils/mock-response.ts:14-16
Title: mock-response.ts doc still claims coverage by a deleted characterization suite

Finding:
This pass resolved the stale references in copy-assets.test.ts and ignore-patterns.test.ts but left the third, most misleading one untouched. src/test-utils/mock-response.ts lines ~14-16 still end the doc block with: 'The behavior is pinned by the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`.' That suite was removed during the refactor (verified: grep finds no 'characterization suite' / 'mockResponse helper' describe block in src/api.test.ts; mockResponse is only used there as an inline setup helper, never characterized). The claim is actively harmful: a maintainer reading the comment will believe mockResponse's edge branches (e.g. the json() fallback-to-empty-object path the comment itself flags as unreached) are regression-pinned when they are not. This is a stale-reference docs bug of exactly the kind flagged in the original review; it just wasn't fixed here.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/test-utils/mock-response.ts, edit the doc comment on the `mockResponse` function. Delete the trailing sentence 'The behavior is pinned by the `mockResponse helper` characterization suite in `src/api.test.ts`.' (and any clause attaching it). Do not leave a dangling reference to a non-existent suite. If you want to keep a note about current coverage, replace it with an accurate statement such as: 'mockResponse is exercised indirectly by the src/api.test.ts request tests; its json() fallback branch is currently not asserted directly.' Verify with `grep -rn "characterization suite" src` and `grep -rn "mockResponse helper" src` that no other stale reference to the deleted suite remains.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The characterization suite in src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts is comprehensive and well-structured — it covers status defaults and passthrough, ok derivation with thorough boundary tests (inclusive 2xx range, off-by-one guards at 199/300), explicit ok override, text() method with all input combinations, json() body-prefers-explicit-body semantics, nullish-body ?? edge cases (null falls through, 0/false/'' preserved), json() text-parsing path including invalid-JSON error, the subtle empty-string truthiness check (opts.text ? ... vs nullish), empty-object fallback, and returned object shape. Tests are independent, have meaningful assertions, and follow project vitest patterns. No tautological, dead, or disabled tests found. The characterization suite adequately pins all documented behavior including the json() fallback branch. However, the doc comment in src/test-utils/mock-response.ts still has the stale reference that the fix was supposed to address.
Attempt 2: The characterization suite at src/test-utils/mock-response.test.ts is comprehensive and pins all documented behavior of mockResponse — status defaults, ok derivation with boundary coverage, explicit ok override, text() and json() methods, nullish-body ?? semantics, empty-string truthiness subtlety, json() fallback-to-empty-object, invalid-JSON error path, and returned object shape. No tautological, dead, or disabled tests. Tests are independent and follow project vitest patterns. However, the doc comment in src/test-utils/mock-response.ts still contains the stale reference that the fix was supposed to remove — the core finding remains unresolved.

## Response Format (required)
Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON object matching the schema below.
- No prose, explanations, or commentary before or after the JSON.
- No markdown code fences (```), no headings — output the raw JSON object.
- Do NOT call any tools and do NOT end your turn on a thinking block. Emit the JSON object directly as text.

JSON schema:
{ approved: Whether the implementation is accepted, feedback: Detailed review feedback, issues: Array<{ file: File with the issue, description: What needs to be fixed, severity: "critical" | "minor" /* How important the fix is */ }> }

/**
 * Tests for `src/app.ts`'s module surface and `startApp` bootstrap: the
 * imperative DOM scaffold it builds, the dialog open/close + shared-widget
 * move wiring, and the two characterization suites that pin app.ts's retained
 * imports and its (dead-)CSS-selector-relevant DOM output.
 *
 * Shared fixtures, DOM scaffolding, and the per-test fetch stub come from
 * `./test-helpers`.
 *
 * Environment: the project-default `happy-dom` (these tests need a DOM).
 *
 * Contract decisions encoded by these suites:
 *  - The action controls Delete / Move / Copy are `<button>` elements whose
 *    trimmed `textContent` is exactly `"Delete"` / `"Move"` / `"Copy"`.
 *  - Importing `./app` must not side-effect when there is no `#app` element.

I'll approve with positive feedback.
